question:
Include string header file in the code like #include<string> and run the following statements
cout << string(2, ' '); //it will print 2 spaces
cout << string(3, '*'); //it will print 3 stars after spaces
Now write a program to print the following pattern using single loop and with only two cout statements (one for the spaces and other for printing the stars on the screen). The hyphen (-) in the pattern are representing spaces. Prompt the user to enter number of lines. Input value of lines is 4 in the example
-	-	-	*	-	-	-
-	-	*	*	*	-	-
-	*	*	*	*	*	-
*	*	*	*	*	*	*

test_cases.expected: Incorrect pattern - spaces increase instead of decrease

errors: Line 11: Uses lines + i - 1 for spaces calculation instead of lines - i - 1, which creates an inverted pattern with increasing spaces instead of decreasing
